title: sedum praealtum
intro: Sedum praealtum, also known as the tall houseleek, is a succulent plant species native to mountainous regions of Central and Eastern Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Sedum praealtum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, Sedum praealtum is often made into infusions, tinctures, or topical creams, although further research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential side effects.
